에스겔 27:1.content: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

에스겔 27:2.content: “You, son of man, take up a lamentation over Tyre;

에스겔 27:3.content: and tell Tyre, ‘You who dwell at the entry of the sea, who are the merchant of the peoples to many islands, the Lord Yahweh says: “You, Tyre, have said, ‘I am perfect in beauty.’

에스겔 27:4.content: Your borders are in the heart of the seas. Your builders have perfected your beauty.

에스겔 27:5.content: They have made all your planks of cypress trees from Senir. They have taken a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.

에스겔 27:6.content: They have made your oars of the oaks of Bashan. They have made your benches of ivory inlaid in cypress wood from the islands of Kittim.

에스겔 27:7.content: Your sail was of fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt, that it might be to you for a banner. Blue and purple from the islands of Elishah was your awning.

에스겔 27:8.content: The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers. Your wise men, Tyre, were in you. They were your pilots.

에스겔 27:9.content: The old men of Gebal and its wise men were your repairers of ship seams in you. All the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to deal in your merchandise.

에스겔 27:10.content: “ ‘ “Persia, Lud, and Put were in your army, your men of war. They hung the shield and helmet in you. They showed your beauty.

에스겔 27:11.content: The men of Arvad with your army were on your walls all around, and valiant men were in your towers. They hung their shields on your walls all around. They have perfected your beauty.

에스겔 27:12.content: “ ‘ “Tarshish was your merchant by reason of the multitude of all kinds of riches. They traded for your wares with silver, iron, tin, and lead.

에스겔 27:13.content: “ ‘ “Javan, Tubal, and Meshech were your traders. They traded the persons of men and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.

에스겔 27:14.content: “ ‘ “They of the house of Togarmah traded for your wares with horses, war horses, and mules.

에스겔 27:15.content: “ ‘ “The men of Dedan traded with you. Many islands were the market of your hand. They brought you horns of ivory and ebony in exchange.

에스겔 27:16.content: “ ‘ “Syria was your merchant by reason of the multitude of your handiworks. They traded for your wares with emeralds, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies.

에스겔 27:17.content: “ ‘ “Judah and the land of Israel were your traders. They traded wheat of Minnith, confections, honey, oil, and balm for your merchandise.

에스겔 27:18.content: “ ‘ “Damascus was your merchant for the multitude of your handiworks, by reason of the multitude of all kinds of riches, with the wine of Helbon, and white wool.

에스겔 27:19.content: “ ‘ “Vedan and Javan traded with yarn for your wares: bright iron, cassia, and calamus were among your merchandise.

에스겔 27:20.content: “ ‘ “Dedan was your trafficker in precious cloths for riding.

에스겔 27:21.content: “ ‘ “Arabia, and all the princes of Kedar were the merchants of your hand; in lambs, rams, and goats. In these, they were your merchants.

에스겔 27:22.content: “ ‘ “The traders of Sheba and Raamah were your traders. They traded for your wares with the chief of all spices, and with all precious stones, and gold.

에스겔 27:23.content: “ ‘ “Haran, Canneh, and Eden, the traders of Sheba, Asshur and Chilmad, were your traders.

에스겔 27:24.content: These were your traders in choice wares, in wrappings of blue and embroidered work, and in chests of rich clothing, bound with cords and made of cedar, among your merchandise.

에스겔 27:25.content: “ ‘ “The ships of Tarshish were your caravans for your merchandise. You were replenished and made very glorious in the heart of the seas.

에스겔 27:26.content: Your rowers have brought you into great waters. The east wind has broken you in the heart of the seas.

에스겔 27:27.content: Your riches, your wares, your merchandise, your mariners, your pilots, your repairers of ship seams, the dealers in your merchandise, and all your men of war, who are in you, with all your company which is among you, will fall into the heart of the seas in the day of your ruin.

에스겔 27:28.content: At the sound of the cry of your pilots, the pasture lands will shake.

에스겔 27:29.content: All who handle the oars, the mariners and all the pilots of the sea, will come down from their ships. They will stand on the land,

에스겔 27:30.content: and will cause their voice to be heard over you, and will cry bitterly. They will cast up dust on their heads. They will wallow in the ashes.

에스겔 27:31.content: They will make themselves bald for you, and clothe themselves with sackcloth. They will weep for you in bitterness of soul, with bitter mourning.

에스겔 27:32.content: In their wailing they will take up a lamentation for you, and lament over you, saying, ‘Who is there like Tyre, like her who is brought to silence in the middle of the sea?’

에스겔 27:33.content: When your wares went out of the seas, you filled many peoples. You enriched the kings of the earth with the multitude of your riches and of your merchandise.

에스겔 27:34.content: In the time that you were broken by the seas, in the depths of the waters, your merchandise and all your company fell within you.

에스겔 27:35.content: All the inhabitants of the islands are astonished at you, and their kings are horribly afraid. They are troubled in their face.

에스겔 27:36.content: The merchants among the peoples hiss at you. You have become a terror, and you will be no more.” ’ ”
